title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid stalled ceasefire talks, Iran rejected a 45-day proposal on April 6, 2026, demanding a permanent end to the war as President Trump's April 7 deadline looms for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with threats of strikes on Iranian infrastructure like bridges and power plants. The conflict, now in its sixth week since US-Israeli airstrikes began in late February, features US air dominance and thousands of targets hit, but no confirmed physical entry of active US military personnel into Iran's terrestrial territory—recent special operations rescues of downed F-15 pilots deep inside Iran were described by Trump as rapid aerial extractions, sparking Polymarket disputes. Regional troop buildups signal escalation risks ahead of the deadline.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France tops the April 2026 FIFA rankings after impressive March friendlies, defeating Brazil 2-1 and Colombia 3-1 to reclaim the No. 1 spot and fuel trader consensus placing them at 13.6% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, just behind Spain's leading 15.9%. Spain holds a slim edge via their Euro 2024 success, youthful core like Lamine Yamal and Pedri, and consistent Nations League form, while defending champions Argentina (9.2%) rely on Lionel Messi's experience despite CONMEBOL qualifying struggles. England (11.3%) and Brazil (8.6%) show strong recent results, including England's draw with Uruguay, but the expanded 48-team format, balanced draw paths, and depth across UEFA and CONMEBOL keep probabilities tightly clustered among elite contenders with minimal separation.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid heightened US-Iran hostilities, including US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian targets for over five weeks and the killing of Iran's intelligence chief, both nations received a draft ceasefire proposal on April 6 via mediators, outlining an immediate halt to fighting, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and 15-20 days for broader terms. Iran rejected prior US 15-point plans as unreasonable, demanding a permanent war end, while President Trump issued an April 8 ultimatum for Hormuz access amid ongoing escalation signals. Diplomatic channels through Pakistan persist, informing trader consensus for de-escalation by late 2026 over nearer deadlines, with the Hormuz standoff as the pivotal near-term catalyst.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.1%, propelled by recent primary polls such as the March POLITICO survey showing him dominating Kamala Harris in California and national surveys from Emerson and YouGov affirming his lead among Democrats, alongside a fresh Washington insider assessment positioning him to lead the party through Trump's second term. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 7.8% on progressive grassroots energy and fundraising strength, Jon Ossoff at 6.7% leverages his Georgia Senate incumbency in a battleground, and Josh Shapiro at 3.8% draws from Pennsylvania gubernatorial success. This wide-open field underscores post-2024 uncertainty; support could consolidate via 2026 midterm performances, donor commitments, and early caucus positioning in Iowa and New Hampshire.

title: Who will Trump talk to in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump between March 1 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's early diplomatic outreach as president amid the US-Iran conflict and NATO burden-sharing debates over Ukraine support drives trader focus on his March 2026 communications with foreign leaders. Verifiable reports confirm a March 27 phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, including Elon Musk, and prior discussions with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, but market outcomes center on specific figures like NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte and Chinese President Xi Jinping. Mid-March phone calls with Rutte surfaced in early April credible reporting on alliance frustrations, yet lack contemporaneous sources, sparking UMA oracle disputes. No Beijing confirmation exists for claimed Xi talks, leaving resolution hinging on consensus standards. With March closed, attention shifts to final oracle rulings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% to win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, ahead of Vice President J.D. Vance at 38% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 22%, reflecting bets on RFK Jr.'s crossover appeal from his independent run and MAHA health reforms amid mixed public approval ratings. This diverges from last week's CPAC straw poll, where Vance led at 53% and Rubio surged to 35% on momentum from GOP donors reportedly favoring him over Vance in Trump queries. Vance faces headwinds from foreign policy scrutiny during U.S.-Iran tensions, while 2026 midterms loom as a key test for the open primary field post-Trump's term limits.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 6?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's elite Augusta National course history, including two green jackets and a recent top-five finish, anchors his trader consensus lead at 14.5% implied probability, bolstered by unmatched ball-striking despite swing tweaks noted at The Players Championship. Yet the market remains tightly bunched, with Jon Rahm (7.8%) and Bryson DeChambeau (7.5%) gaining traction on dominant LIV Golf form—Rahm conceding strokes to just seven players across five starts—while Rory McIlroy (6.5%) carries defending champion momentum and Ludvig Aberg (5.8%) impresses with rising major contention. Withdrawals like Phil Mickelson and Tommy Fleetwood for injuries, plus Collin Morikawa's Texas Open pullout limiting reps, underscore the wide-open 91-player field where upsets thrive amid variable spring conditions.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge over California Governor Gavin Newsom in prediction market odds for the 2028 presidential winner, reflecting trader consensus on their frontrunner status amid early jockeying. Vance's lead stems from his recent CPAC straw poll victory among Republicans, appearance at a major conservative donor summit, and new book release fueling speculation as a Trump successor, while Newsom tops recent Democratic leadership surveys and California primary polls. Secretary of State Marco Rubio trails at third, boosted by his high-profile role in ongoing Iran tensions where he diverges from Vance. The race remains tightly contested with probabilities under 20% due to its early stage—over two years pre-primaries—and vulnerability to 2026 midterm results, administration policy outcomes, scandals, or Trump's endorsement, any of which could sharply separate contenders.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen has surged to frontrunner status at 36.4% implied probability on Polymarket following their landslide UMK national final victory on February 28, nearly tripling the runner-up's score with a high-energy rock-classical-dance fusion that's dominated OGAE fan votes, prediction models, and bookmaker odds. Trader consensus reflects universal acclaim for its broad appeal to both juries and televoters, bolstered by recent official music video releases and first semi-final running order reveal positioning them favorably on May 12. France trails at 10.9% as a safe Big Five auto-finalist with Monroe's "Regarde!", while Denmark (10.4%), Greece's Akylas ("Ferto"), and Australia's Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") gain from strong national selections and streaming buzz, though all face semi-final qualification risks amid tightening competition ahead of Vienna's May 16 grand final.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions, driven by their league-best 62-16 record, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner status and win shares lead, plus a fully healthy roster entering the playoffs after coasting through the regular season on a hot streak. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 16.6% following their 59-19 mark and Victor Wembanyama's postseason dominance potential, as noted by scouts, despite a recent overtime loss to Denver on April 4. Boston Celtics (12%) and Nuggets (9.5%) benefit from playoff experience and stars like Jayson Tatum and Nikola Jokić, who tops rebounds and assists, while Detroit Pistons (5.2%) top the East at 57-21 amid a tight conference race with few games left.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 98.2% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting robust March 2026 nonfarm payrolls that added 178,000 jobs—far exceeding the 60,000 consensus—and steady February CPI inflation at 2.4% year-over-year, aligning with the Fed's 2% target trajectory. The March FOMC held the fed funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, with dot plot projections for just one cut later in 2026 amid resilient labor conditions and cooling wage growth to 3.5% annualized. This skin-in-the-game consensus underscores economic stability, though hotter-than-expected March CPI data due April 10 or escalating energy prices from geopolitical tensions could spark minor rate hike pricing.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship with 72 points after back-to-back Grand Prix wins in China and Japan, becoming the youngest leader ever at 19, capitalizing on a timely safety car in Suzuka while teammate George Russell suffered a battery failure that dropped him from podium contention. Mercedes' dominant W12 chassis has propelled the duo to the top, leading constructors by 45 points over Ferrari, with Russell's 63 points from the Australia victory and Shanghai Sprint underscoring his consistency. Traders price Russell's experience ahead in the internal title fight (43.5% implied probability vs. Antonelli's 31.1%), despite the nine-point gap, as Toto Wolff signals potential team orders later amid no major injuries or reliability crises reported post-Japan on March 29. Charles Leclerc trails at 49 points for Ferrari, reflecting their constructors' deficit.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have driven WTI crude oil futures above $112 per barrel as of April 6, 2026, marking a 4-6% surge this week amid fears of supply disruptions from Middle East producers and potential Iran-related escalations. This risk premium overshadows bearish U.S. crude inventories, which rose to near three-year highs in the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, alongside OPEC+'s modest 206,000 bpd production adjustment for April. Global demand worries persist from softening economic data, but trader consensus prices in elevated volatility through month-end. Key catalysts include the April 8 EIA report, IEA Oil Market Report, and any U.S.-Iran negotiation outcomes, with May WTI futures implying sustained pressure around $112.

title: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Punjab Kings
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Punjab Kings scheduled for April 6 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Punjab Kings - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Punjab Kings scheduled for 2026-04-06 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Punjab Kings - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Punjab Kings scheduled for 2026-04-06 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Punjab Kings enter as trader favorites against a depleted Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens, buoyed by two opening wins that vaulted them near the IPL points table summit with a positive net run rate, while KKR sit winless after back-to-back losses amid bowling woes. Critical absences hamstring KKR's attack: Sunil Narine sidelined by illness, Varun Chakravarthy nursing a hand injury from their last outing, and Harshit Rana ruled out long-term with knee surgery recovery, leaving their spin and pace options thin against PBKS's in-form batting. KKR hold a dominant 21-13 head-to-head edge, but rain interruptions and a batting-friendly pitch add uncertainty, with weather forecasts pivotal for any full innings or potential washout sharing points.

title: LoL: GIANTX vs Team Heretics (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between GIANTX and Team Heretics in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 6 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "GIANTX" if GIANTX win the match against Team Heretics.

This market will resolve to "Team Heretics" if Team Heretics win the match against GIANTX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both GIANTX and Team Heretics each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both GIANTX and Team Heretics each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both GIANTX and Team Heretics each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both GIANTX and Team Heretics. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: GIANTX leads LEC Spring 2026 standings at 3-0 series (6-2 games), bolstered by a 2-0 sweep of SK Gaming yesterday and prior 2-1 win over Team Heretics earlier this split, driving trader consensus toward their favoritism in this BO3 rematch under Fearless Draft rules. Team Heretics languishes at 1-3 (3-7 games) amid a three-series skid, including 2-0 losses to G2 Esports and Team Vitality last week, despite new Korean support Way's debut yielding a 2-1 upset over SK on March 29. GIANTX's momentum and head-to-head edge contrast Heretics' integration struggles and bottom-table pressure for playoff positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons (57-21), atop the Eastern Conference standings and locked into playoff position, face the Orlando Magic (42-36) on the road in a late-season matchup with playoff seeding implications for the hosts. Critical injury updates from today's official NBA report have created uncertainty: Pistons star Cade Cunningham is doubtful with a left lung issue after missing 10 games, joined by Isaiah Stewart (doubtful, calf), Tobias Harris (doubtful, knee), Duncan Robinson (doubtful, hip), and Caris LeVert (questionable, knee). Magic counter without Franz Wagner (ankle, back-to-back), Anthony Black (abdomen), Jonathan Isaac (knee), and Jett Howard (ankle), thinning their depth at home. Pistons' recent depth-fueled wins without Cunningham and historical edge in the series (leading 3-1 this season) shape trader consensus toward Detroit despite the travel and injury flux, while Orlando's urgency for play-in security adds upset potential.

title: Elon Musk # tweets March 31 - April 7, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket pins Elon Musk's X posts from March 31 to April 7 at 260-279 (52.5% implied probability), driven by his consistent high-volume cadence averaging 33 daily posts so far, with 231 logged through 86% of the period per real-time trackers. A recent dip to 66 posts over April 4-6 (22 per day) followed a 73-post spike on April 3 fueled by viral political commentary on California Medicaid fraud and Tesla Full Self-Driving safety stats, tempering upward momentum but keeping projections steady. The closely contested 280-299 bin (32.5%) hinges on April 7's final engagement surge, amid ongoing buzz from Tesla sales slumps and SpaceX updates that amplify his pop culture dominance.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Israel again in March?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of the listed country between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the 2026 Iran war that erupted on February 28 with U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian nuclear sites, missile production facilities, and military leadership, Iran retaliated with multiple ballistic missile and drone barrages targeting Israel and U.S. bases in the Middle East, including volleys of Khorramshahr-4 and Fattah hypersonic missiles by late March—confirming military action well before the March 31 deadline. Trader consensus reflected this escalation, driven by Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure and proxy attacks via Hezbollah. Over the past week, exchanges intensified with 38 Iranian missiles hitting Israel on April 1, Israel striking IRGC sites, and President Trump extending a Hormuz reopening ultimatum to April 7 amid rejected ceasefire proposals.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 64.5% to become Hungary's next prime minister following the April 12 parliamentary election, reflecting independent polls showing his centre-right Tisza party leading Viktor Orbán's Fidesz by widening margins among decided voters—such as 56%-37% (21 Research Centre, March 23-28) and 51%-38% (Závecz Research, March 24-28), per recent Reuters reporting. This surge stems from grassroots mobilization, youth turnout, economic discontent, frozen EU funds, and corruption allegations against the incumbent government, contrasting Magyar's pro-EU/NATO stance. Fidesz retains 34.5% implied probability due to the gerrymandered electoral system favoring individual constituencies and diaspora votes, with government-aligned polls depicting a tighter race. Post-election coalition talks will finalize the government formation.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US-Israel war against Iran, launched with airstrikes on February 28, entered day 38 on April 6 as Tehran rejected a US- and mediator-proposed 45-day ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end amid ongoing hostilities over the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump's extended ultimatum—due within hours—threatens intensified strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, following Israel's recent attack on a major petrochemical site in the South Pars gas field that produces half of Iran's output. Missile barrages continue, with Iran targeting Israeli cities like Haifa and US forces reporting downed jets, while diplomatic efforts involving Egypt and Qatar have stalled. Traders watch for escalation signals or breakthroughs before Trump's deadline, amid no-confidence in short-term de-escalation.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 4 - April 6, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 4 12:00 PM ET to April 6, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post <40 tweets from April 4 to April 6, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 4 12:00 PM ET to April 6, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs the 65-89 tweet range at virtually 100% implied probability for Elon Musk's X activity from April 4, 12:00 PM ET to April 6, 12:00 PM ET, driven by the live tracker showing exactly 66 main feed posts, quotes, and reposts so far—replies excluded unless on the main feed. With mere hours remaining until market close at noon ET on this final day, Musk's steady pace of around 33 posts daily has not accelerated, tempered by reports of travel to San Jose and sleep patterns, locking in the outcome absent a dramatic surge. An upset to 90+ would require 24+ posts in the closing window, improbable given his recent morning activity and historical bursts rarely exceeding 40 daily amid business demands.

title: Lightning vs. Sabres
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 6 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning (48-22-6) face Buffalo Sabres (46-23-8) tonight at KeyBank Center in a pivotal Atlantic Division matchup with playoff seeding on the line. Sabres hold a 2-0-1 edge this season, including an 8-7 thriller on March 8 featuring 102 penalty minutes and 15 total goals. Lightning blue-liner Victor Hedman remains sidelined on LTIR with a long-term injury, thinning their defense, while Sabres forward Sam Carrick is week-to-week after a left arm injury from March 31, prompting lineup adjustments. Buffalo aims to snap a recent form dip at home; Tampa opens its final regular-season road trip. Home-ice advantage and head-to-head trends inform trader consensus.

title: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Valentin Vacherot vs Juan Manuel Cerundolo
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Valentin Vacherot and Juan Manuel Cerundolo in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 6 at 9:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Valentin Vacherot' if Valentin Vacherot advances against Juan Manuel Cerundolo.

This market will resolve to 'Juan Manuel Cerundolo' if Juan Manuel Cerundolo advances against Valentin Vacherot.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valentin Vacherot, the No. 23-ranked Monegasque at a career high, faces qualifier Juan Manuel Cerundolo (No. 69) in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters first round on slow clay, where home crowd support and Vacherot's career 142-78 clay record fuel trader optimism despite his untested 2026 surface form after a Miami fourth-round exit to Arthur Fils. Cerundolo, an Argentine baseline grinder, brings momentum from decisive qualifier wins over Hugo Nys (6-3, 6-3) and Pedro Martinez (6-2, 2-6, 6-3) in the past 48 hours, backing his 8-5 clay mark this year. With no head-to-head, stylistic matchup favors endurance on this grind-heavy court, though Vacherot's recent hard-court wins over Mariano Navone and Matteo Berrettini highlight his rising class.

title: Counter-Strike: PARIVISION vs FUT Esports (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between PARIVISION and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against PARIVISION.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: FUT (-1.5) vs PARIVISION (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between PARIVISION and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 6 at 11:10AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports wins 2 or more maps than PARIVISION in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "PARIVISION". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: PARIVISION (#5 HLTV) and FUT Esports (#13 HLTV), both 2-0 in PGL Bucharest 2026 Swiss group stage round 3, clash in a pivotal BO3 with the winner advancing toward playoffs. PARIVISION's edge stems from yesterday's 2-0 rout of FOKUS, strong map win rates on Ancient (82%) and Dust2 (65%), and a 2-1 head-to-head victory over FUT last month at PGL Cluj-Napoca, fueled by Jame's elite AWP play (1.14 rating) and BELCHONOKK's fire form. FUT counters with momentum from 2-0 over NRG, 4th at ESL Pro League S23 Finals (upsets over MOUZ, G2), and dominance on Mirage (75%) and Nuke (80%). No roster changes; stylistic matchup favors PARIVISION's structure, but FUT's aggression poses upset risk in standard CS2 map pool.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the PGL Bucharest 2026 group stage Swiss round 3, BC.Game Esports and Voca clash in a high-stakes BO3 elimination match, both sitting at 0-2 records after day-two losses—BC.Game fell 0-2 to The MongolZ and 1-2 to MIBR in a tense Anubis decider despite leading 9-4, while Voca dropped 0-2 to NRG and 1-2 to 3DMAX. BC.Game's star-laden roster featuring s1mple, electroNic, and IGL MUTiRiS boasts superior firepower and map edges on Overpass (67% win rate) and Nuke (75%), bolstered by recent coach TaZ's influence amid January roster refresh. Voca, HLTV #50 with NA roots, counters via junior and Jeorge's form from qualifier triumphs like Fragadelphia York, excelling on Dust2 (73%) and Mirage (83%). No head-to-head history; trader consensus leans BC.Game on pedigree despite shaky starts, with momentum pivotal for playoff survival.

title: # of views of MrBeast video day 2?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the latest YouTube video posted by MrBeast gets in the first 48 hours after being posted. This market may not resolve until the 48 hours are complete, regardless of whether a strike is reached earlier.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to the video titled "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000", which can be found here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DXVHmGoCTco. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: MrBeast's "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000" exploded out of the gate with 34 million YouTube views in its first 24 hours after the April 4 premiere, propelled by star-studded collaborations with top streamers like IShowSpeed, xQc, and Pokimane, driving massive cross-platform buzz. By hour 40, views surpassed 50.8 million, aligning with the channel's historical pattern of 15-20 million additional day-two gains on high-stakes challenge videos, reinforced by a live finale stream peaking at 1 million concurrent viewers. Trader consensus at 100% implied probability for 50-53 million in 48 hours reflects this steady ~1.2 million hourly velocity and skin-in-the-game momentum. Realistic upsets would require abrupt algorithm shifts or competing viral drops to dip below 50 million, or reaction video surges to exceed 53 million—both low-likelihood given current trajectory.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including late March surveys from Paraná Pesquisas and BTG Pactual/Nexus, show President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding a narrow first-round lead of 2-6 points over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, but the two are tied or Flávio ahead by 1-3 points in simulated runoffs, mirroring Polymarket's tight trader consensus with Lula at 41.5% and Flávio at 38.4% for outright victory. This polarization stems from right-wing consolidation behind Flávio as heir to his father Jair Bolsonaro's base—ineligible after two terms—while Lula, seeking a fourth non-consecutive term, faces voter fatigue amid economic headwinds. Lula's March 31 confirmation of Geraldo Alckmin as running mate bolsters his coalition, but Flávio's surging polls grant leeway on economic team announcements. With the October 4 first round six months away, shifts could arise from fresh polling, endorsements, scandals, or macroeconomic data in Brazil's two-round electoral system.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atalanta BC's implied 100% probability reflects their commanding 3-0 victory over US Lecce in Serie A at Via del Mare, confirmed by official scoresheets, solidifying trader consensus post-match. Pre-kickoff sentiment stemmed from Lecce's crippling injury crisis—absent Lassana Coulibaly (hamstring), Kialonda Gaspar (inner ligament), Medon Berisha (torn thigh muscle), Riccardo Sottil (lumbago), and Lameck Banda—leaving them vulnerable in the relegation scrap. Atalanta, buoyed by strong recent form and a top-four push, overcame Isak Hien's international injury and Gianluca Scamacca's thigh doubt to dominate, extending their head-to-head edge (14 wins vs. Lecce's 8). No appeals or irregularities alter this outcome.
